title: Kochia tripteris
taxonomy: Kochia tripteris is classified within the family Amaranthaceae, commonly known as the amaranth or pigweed family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristics, which include its branching stems and small, inconspicuous flowers. The genus Kochia is further divided into several species, with Kochia tripteris being one of the most notable. Members of the Amaranthaceae family are often found in dry, sandy areas and have adapted to thrive in such environments. This family is also notable for its diverse range of species, including both annual and perennial plants.
